invalidation.condition: Eaton Electrical segment backlog declines more than 20% YoY for 2 consecutive quarterly earnings reports AND Schneider Electric backlog also declines YoY in the same periods.
invalidation.rationale: Simultaneous backlog decline at both Eaton AND Schneider would signal broad-based demand destruction in grid equipment — not a company-specific issue. A single company decline is noise; simultaneous decline at two major players is signal.

invalidation.condition: Power transformer delivery lead times fall below 8 months per 2 consecutive quarterly Wood Mackenzie surveys.
invalidation.rationale: Below 8 months = supply normalized to pre-2020 levels; the physical bottleneck that drives equipment pricing power has resolved. Reduce to 2% and reassess whether demand growth alone justifies the position without the supply-constraint amplifier.

invalidation.condition: US Federal Reserve raises rates to entry_rate + 200bps within 18 months of entry.
invalidation.rationale: A +200bps rate shock would compress utility P/E multiples enough to dominate the earnings-growth signal in IQQH.DE. Reduce to 2% and hold equipment-pure exposure only; re-enter at full size when rate peak is confirmed.

invalidation.condition: IQQH.DE falls more than 20% below entry price for 10 consecutive trading days.
invalidation.rationale: A 20% drawdown triggers a review, not an automatic exit. Check whether the decline is thesis-related (backlog deterioration, rate shock) or market-wide. If market-wide and fundamentals intact, hold or add. Only exit if combined with a triggered fundamental invalidation.

assumptions.statement: Eaton Electrical sector backlog YoY growth stays positive (above 0%) in each quarterly earnings report during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: Eaton Corp quarterly earnings (SEC Form 8-K, backlog is a reported segment metric). Falsified by: Eaton Electrical backlog reports negative YoY growth for 2 consecutive quarters.
assumptions.status_note: Q1 2026: Eaton Electrical backlog +48% YoY to $14.5B. 2026 organic growth guidance raised to 10%. Record first quarter.

assumptions.statement: Power transformer delivery lead times remain above 12 months per Wood Mackenzie / S&P Global quarterly survey.
assumptions.measurability: Source: Wood Mackenzie quarterly transformer survey. Falsified by: two consecutive quarterly reports showing lead times below 12 months.
assumptions.status_note: Most recent WoodMac (Q1 2026): 20 months. Prior: 22 months. Still 67% above threshold.

assumptions.statement: Combined hyperscaler AI capex guidance (Microsoft + Google + Amazon + Meta) stays above $100B/quarter during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: quarterly earnings transcripts and 10-Q/10-K. Falsified by: any single quarter where combined guidance falls below $100B AND represents a >30% sequential cut.
assumptions.status_note: Q1 2026: ~$700B annual = $175B/quarter average. All 4 hyperscalers raised guidance. Threshold not at risk.

assumptions.statement: US Federal Reserve does not raise rates more than 100bps cumulative above the entry-date level during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: FOMC rate decisions. Falsified by: Fed funds target rises to entry_rate + 100bps or more at any FOMC meeting during the horizon.
assumptions.status_note: Fed in easing/hold cycle (2024–2026). No hike signals in near-term guidance. Macro override-risk monitor, not an active concern.

risk_discipline.note: Migrated from legacy thesis_20260603_grid_infrastructure_utilities_bindingconstraint (v2). Full position €500, bought on the dip 2026-06-04 — no rebalance. Re-rating mechanism: quarterly order-book data (Eaton/Schneider/ABB, Jul 2026) → earnings upgrades → P/E re-rating. Horizon 12–78 weeks.

metadata.attribution_note: Weights are migration estimates: thesis names struct_energy_transition_grid as primary and struct_ai_capex_supercycle as an independent secondary demand driver → 0.7/0.3.
metadata.fill_note: €500 full amount, bought on the dip 2026-06-05 (IQQH.DE, EUR). amount_eur=500 authoritative; actual fill price €11.27 → qty 44.37 (user-confirmed 2026-06-06; current ≈€10.53).
